fields.label: Store Name
fields.description: Friendly name for this store, e.g. 'Not Drones India'. Used as the record identifier.

fields.label: Shop Domain
fields.description: Your Shopify store domain, e.g. notdrones.myshopify.com — must match the X-Shopify-Shop-Domain header sent in webhooks.

fields.label: Enable Sync
fields.description: Master switch. When off, incoming webhooks from this store are ignored.

fields.label: Webhook Secret
fields.description: Signing secret from Shopify Admin → Settings → Notifications → Webhooks. Used to verify that incoming requests are genuinely from Shopify.

fields.label: Company
fields.description: All documents created from this store will be posted under this company.

fields.label: Default Warehouse
fields.description: Default stock location for items on Sales Orders created from this store.

fields.label: Company Address
fields.description: Your company's billing address — printed on Sales Orders.

fields.label: Dispatch Address
fields.description: Address goods are dispatched from. India Compliance uses this to determine intra-state vs inter-state GST. Leave blank to fall back to Company Address.

fields.label: Sales Order Series
fields.description: Naming series applied to Sales Orders created from this store.

fields.label: Price List
fields.description: Price list to attach to Sales Orders. Rates are always taken directly from the Shopify order — the price list is used for reference only.

fields.label: Customer Series
fields.description: Naming series for new Customers auto-created from Shopify orders. Leave blank to use the ERPNext default.

fields.label: Default Customer Group
fields.description: Customer Group assigned to new customers created from Shopify orders.

fields.label: Default Territory
fields.description: Territory assigned to new customers and Sales Orders from this store.

fields.label: Payment Terms
fields.description: Select a Payment Terms Template to apply based on the Shopify order's payment status. Leave blank to create Sales Orders without payment terms.

fields.label: Paid Orders
fields.description: Applied when Shopify financial_status = 'paid'.

fields.label: Partially Paid Orders
fields.description: Applied when Shopify financial_status = 'partially_paid'.

fields.label: Pending / Unpaid Orders
fields.description: Applied when Shopify financial_status = 'pending', 'voided', or 'unpaid'.

fields.label: Draft Behaviour
fields.description: By default all Sales Orders are submitted automatically. Enable these to keep specific order types as Draft for manual review before submission.

fields.label: Keep Draft — Paid
fields.description: Sales Orders for 'paid' Shopify orders will be saved as Draft instead of submitted. Payment Entry cannot be created for draft Sales Orders.

fields.label: Keep Draft — Partially Paid
fields.description: Sales Orders for 'partially_paid' Shopify orders will be saved as Draft instead of submitted.

fields.label: Keep Draft — Pending / Unpaid
fields.description: Sales Orders for 'pending', 'voided', or 'unpaid' Shopify orders will be saved as Draft instead of submitted.

fields.label: Accounting Dimensions
fields.description: These dimensions are stamped on every Sales Order and Payment Entry created from this store.

fields.label: Shipping
fields.description: The ERPNext item used to represent Shopify shipping charges as a line item on the Sales Order.

fields.label: Shipping Item
fields.description: Must have an Item Tax Template so GST is stripped correctly from Shopify's tax-inclusive shipping price.

fields.label: GST
fields.description:
When a GSTIN is found at the configured path in the Shopify order, the integration will:
1. Use the GST-registered legal name as the customer name.
2. Use the GST-registered address as the billing address (looked up locally first, then fetched from India Compliance portal if available).
3. Leave the shipping address exactly as provided by Shopify.

fields.label: GSTIN Shopify Field Path
fields.description: Dot-notation path in the Shopify order payload where the customer's GSTIN is stored. e.g. billing_address.company. The value is validated against the standard GSTIN format — non-GSTIN values at this path are ignored. Leave blank to disable GST-based resolution.

fields.label: Settings
fields.description: When enabled, a Payment Entry is automatically created for every Shopify order with payment status Paid or Partially Paid.

fields.label: Auto-Submit Payment Entry
fields.description: Uncheck to save Payment Entries as Draft for manual review before submission.

fields.label: Payment Entry Series
fields.description: Naming series for Payment Entries created from this store. Leave blank to use the default.

fields.label: Default Fallback Account
fields.description: Used when no row in the Gateway Mapping table matches the incoming order's payment gateway.

fields.label: Bank / Cash Account
fields.description: Ledger account (type: Bank or Cash) that receives the payment.

fields.label: Gateway → Account Mapping
fields.description:
Map each Shopify payment gateway to an ERPNext Bank / Cash Account. Rows are evaluated top-down — first match wins.

Tag Contains (higher priority) — matches if the order's tags field contains this text (case-insensitive). Use this when your payment app registers orders as 'manual' but tags them 'CASHFREE', 'RAZORPAY', etc.
Shopify Gateway — matches against payment_gateway_names[0] in the webhook. Exact match first, then similarity-based fallback.

Tip: open any Shopify Log → Raw Payload to see the exact payment_gateway_names and tags your store sends.

fields.label: Settings
fields.description:
Automatically create a Sales Invoice after the Sales Order is processed. Works with or without Payment Entry.

After Payment Entry — Invoice is created right after the Payment Entry succeeds. If Payment Entry is disabled, the Invoice is created immediately after Sales Order submission.
After Delivery Note — Invoice is created from the Delivery Note. Use the Delivery Note Timing section below to choose between Immediate (on DN submit) or Scheduled (hourly, with optional delay).

fields.label: Create Invoice
fields.options:
After Payment Entry
After Delivery Note

fields.label: Auto-Submit Sales Invoice
fields.description: Uncheck to save Sales Invoices as Draft for manual review before submission.

fields.label: Delivery Note Timing
fields.description:
Controls when the Sales Invoice is created after a Delivery Note is submitted.

Immediate — A background job is enqueued the moment the DN is submitted. The invoice appears within seconds.
Scheduled — The hourly scheduler creates the invoice at the next run. Use Delay After Submission to impose a minimum wait before the scheduler acts.

fields.label: Delay After Submission (Hours)
fields.description: Minimum hours after Delivery Note submission before the scheduler creates the invoice. Set 0 to create at the next hourly run with no delay.

fields.label: Sales Invoice Series
fields.description: Naming series for Sales Invoices created from this store. Leave blank to use the default.

fields.label: Credit Note
fields.description:
When a Shopify refund webhook (refunds/create) arrives, create a Credit Note (return Sales Invoice) against the original Sales Invoice.

Auto — Credit Note is created automatically in the background.
Manual — The refund is logged; create the Credit Note yourself in ERPNext.

Prerequisite: Sales Invoice creation must be enabled on the Sales Invoice tab.

fields.label: Enable Credit Note Creation
fields.description: Requires Sales Invoice creation to be enabled.

fields.label: Credit Note Creation
fields.description: How to handle incoming Shopify refunds.

fields.label: Auto-Submit Credit Note
fields.description: Uncheck to save Credit Notes as Draft for manual review before submission.

fields.label: Credit Note Series
fields.description: Naming series for Credit Notes created from this store. Leave blank to use the ERPNext default.

fields.label: e-Invoice & e-Waybill (India GST)
fields.description:
Requires the India Compliance app. After the Sales Invoice is submitted, automatically trigger e-Invoice and/or e-Waybill generation via the IRP portal.

Each document is processed in a separate background job — a portal error or timeout never blocks or reverses the Sales Invoice.

Prerequisites:
1. Sales Invoice creation must be enabled on the Sales Invoice tab.
2. Auto-Submit Sales Invoice must be enabled — e-Invoice and e-Waybill can only be generated for submitted documents.

India Compliance handles per-company eligibility internally (applicable companies list, turnover threshold, B2B/B2C) — ineligible invoices are skipped silently.

fields.label: Generate e-Invoice
fields.description: Calls generate_e_invoice() from India Compliance after the Sales Invoice is submitted. IRN is registered with the IRP and stored on the Sales Invoice.

fields.label: Generate e-Waybill
fields.description:
Calls generate_e_waybill() from India Compliance after the Sales Invoice is submitted.

After Delivery Note trigger: The Delivery Note is already created before the SI, so transporter details (Vehicle No, LR No, Transporter ID) are available. If those fields are filled on the DN, India Compliance includes Part B automatically — no manual step needed.

After Payment Entry trigger: The SI is created before any Delivery Note, so the shipment hasn't been dispatched yet. Part B (transporter / vehicle details) must be updated manually on the Sales Invoice once the shipment is assigned.

fields.label: e-Waybill Threshold (₹)
fields.description: Minimum invoice value for e-Waybill generation. Leave blank to follow the threshold configured in GST Settings. Set this to avoid generating e-Waybills for low-value orders if GST Settings is misconfigured.

fields.label: Failure Alerts
fields.description:
Email addresses to notify when automation fails. Covers three scenarios:
1. Sales Order creation failure — any error during order processing.
2. Payment Entry failure — Sales Order created but payment failed; requires manual attention.
3. Sales Invoice failure — Sales Order or Delivery Note processed but invoice creation failed; requires manual attention.

fields.label: To
fields.description: Comma-separated list of recipients.

fields.label: CC
fields.description: Comma-separated list of addresses to CC.

fields.label: Log Management
fields.description: Shopify Logs are created for every incoming webhook. Use the retention setting to automatically clean up old logs and keep the database lean.

fields.label: Delete Shopify Logs After (Days)
fields.description: Automatically delete Shopify Logs older than this many days. Leave blank or 0 to keep all logs.

fields.label: Custom Field Mapping
fields.description:
Map any field from the Shopify order payload to a field on an ERPNext document.

Any standard or custom Sales Order field can be a target — e.g. po_no, customer_gstin, custom_shopify_note. Only internal system fields that control document identity or state are blocked (name, docstatus, owner, etc.).

Use dot-notation for nested Shopify values, e.g. billing_address.company or customer.email.
